receiptStandards.description: Clear and readable receipts must be submitted
receiptStandards.rule: Clear and readable receipts and invoices must be submitted with expenses

receiptStandards.description: Must be actual tax receipts or invoices
receiptStandards.rule: Documents must be actual tax receipts or invoices; booking confirmations will not suffice
Online copies are sufficient, a hard copy is not required

receiptStandards.description: Local Employer company name must appear on invoices
receiptStandards.rule: Invoices must show "Global People DE GmbH" not the worker's name

receiptStandards.description: Local Employer address must appear on invoices
Local Employer VAT ID must appear on invoices
Worker or company name can appear on invoices
receiptStandards.rule: The worker or the company's name can appear on the invoice or supporting documents

receiptStandards.description: Local Employer name required for tax-free reimbursement
receiptStandards.rule: Under certain Local employers, if invoices are not issued in the Local Employers name, then tax free reimbursement is not possible

receiptStandards.description: Local Employer company name must appear on invoices
receiptStandards.rule: Invoices must show "GoGlobal Germany GmbH" not the worker's name

receiptStandards.description: Local Employer address must appear on invoices
Local Employer company name must appear on invoices
receiptStandards.rule: Invoices must show "Parakar Germany GmbH" not the worker's name

receiptStandards.description: Local Employer address must appear on invoices
Worker name must appear on hotel/flight invoices
receiptStandards.rule: Hotel/flights invoices should indicate the name of the worker not the company

receiptStandards.description: Invoices over 150 EUR must include employer details
receiptStandards.rule: If invoices are over €150, are required to note the employer's name, address and VAT amount

receiptStandards.description: Mobile phone invoices must include company name
receiptStandards.rule: Any mobile phone invoice that does not include "Parakar Germany GmbH" (at least in c/o) cannot be reimbursed

receiptStandards.description: Home internet invoices must include company name
receiptStandards.rule: Any home internet invoice that does not include "Parakar Germany GmbH" (at least in c/o) cannot be reimbursed

receiptStandards.description: Invoices over 450 EUR must include additional information
receiptStandards.rule: Invoices exceeding gross amount of EUR 450 must contain: worker name and address, tax/VAT ID of restaurant, invoice serial number, invoice date and meal date, net amount, applicable tax rate and VAT

compliancePoliciesGrossUpRelated.grossUpRule: Business expenses related to workers completing their job will usually be tax exempt. Only purely business related elements will be tax free, anything additional will be grossed up
Office equipment (business use) is tax exempt
Training (job-related) is tax exempt
Phone/internet up to €20/month max is tax exempt. Amounts above €20/month will be grossed up
Home office up to €6/day, max €1,260/year is tax exempt. Amounts above these limits will be grossed up
Wellness benefits up to max €600/year are tax exempt. Amounts above €600/year will be grossed up
IT equipment that is considered as company property is tax exempt
Office supplies that are relevant and reasonable in amount are tax exempt
Meals (outside of the business travel report) are not tax exempt and will be grossed up
Fuel is not tax exempt and will be grossed up
Phone Bill is not tax exempt and will be grossed up
Transportation to the workplace is not tax exempt and will be grossed up
Office groceries are not tax exempt and will be grossed up
Other items which are not essential to carry out work activity are not tax exempt and will be grossed up

Entertainment expenses are tax exempt only when offered to a third party and invoices note Global People's name, address and VAT amount. Entertainment expenses solely for employees of same company will be grossed up
Telephone costs up to 20% of invoiced amount, max EUR 20 per month is tax exempt. Amounts above these limits will be grossed up
Mobile phone usage is tax exempt only if worker uses separate phone for personal use with proof. Without proof will be grossed up
Internet costs up to 25% of invoice amount is tax exempt. Amounts above 25% will be grossed up
Personal meals cannot be reimbursed tax-free and will be grossed up
Based on three month record an average percentage rate is determined on how much private cell phone is used for business purposes - this is tax exempt
Mileage reimbursement at €0.30 per km (standard tax-free rate) is tax exempt
KM reimbursement will be tax exempt but fuel will be grossed up
Per diem within legal rates (€14/day over 8h, €28/day for 24h, €14 arrival/departure days) is tax exempt. Amounts above legal rates will be grossed up

Per diem within government rates per country is tax exempt for up to three months of continuous business travel in a given location. Per diems above government rates or after three months will be grossed up. Long stays (more than three days in a week) extending beyond three months are taxable
As a general rule, reimbursements related to travel expenses are tax exempt

compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.additionalInfoRule: Receipts and invoices must be submitted with expenses
Receipts should be submitted with the same currency and clear exchange rate
Expenses can be submitted through Expensify or manual expense report (to be agreed between worker and manager)
Detailed context must be provided so Global People can identify tax free expenses
One consolidated report per employee must be submitted
Expense details must include: type of expense, context, copy of the invoice
Training expenses require the approval of the direct manager
Original invoices, although stored digitally, need to be kept for 10 years
Description of the reason for the expense should be as precise and detailed as possible
Parakar only accepts expenses that are properly documented by means of invoices or receipts from the provider, that specify the service provided
Mobile phone usage requires proof that worker uses separate phone for personal use
All travel related expenses must be reported using Travel Expense Report.xlsx template
Business travel expenses must be submitted using the travel report template
Business trip - submit a separate report for each trip
Supporting documents required to ensure flight reimbursement is processed as net: missing payment receipt/invoice as well as ticket copy
An A1 certificate is required when travelling
Per diem method cannot be mixed with actual expenses. You will need to agree with the worker on one method per business trip
Per diem method cannot be mixed with actual expenses. You will need to agree with the worker on one method per business trip

compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.expenseType: Domestic business travel per diem with provided meals
compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.additionalInfoRule: If meals have been pre-arranged the meal allowance should be reduced by 20 to 40% depending on which meals were provided

compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.expenseType: International business travel per diem with provided meals
compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.additionalInfoRule: When meals are offered at no cost, the daily meal allowance will be reduced by 20 to 40% depending on which meals were provided

compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.expenseType: Domestic business travel per diem with all meals provided
compliancePoliciesAdditionalInfoRelated.additionalInfoRule: For domestic business trips, the total allowance per day is reduced to zero when all meals (breakfast, lunch and dinner) are provided
No receipts or invoices need to be provided when using the per diem method
Mileage reimbursement requires providing a Fahrtenbuch (mileage logbook), which must include date, route, purpose, and odometer readings
Worker will need to share a map with the relevant route (google maps is sufficient) and complete the travel report where applicable
Expense reimbursements will be either processed along the salary and reflected in worker's pay slip or paid out separately, depending on local employer policy
